_meta: AccentAuthor v0 PROPOSAL — not canonical. Deliverable 2 of DISPATCH_accent_drill.md. Frames + declared axes for the mechanical variant pipeline (DECISIONS 2026-07-20 A(a)); the pipeline supplies volume, I sample-review. Taxonomy ratified 2026-07-21 (AccentAuthor_Architecture_taxonomy v3). Index-scored throughout: NO substring marking, so criterion 18/19 substring hazards do not arise.
author: AccentAuthor
topic_short_note: RESOLVED 2026-07-21 (Smith): use 'accents' (matches the tree root; the word reads more naturally than an abbreviation). No marking impact — id prefix only.

outcome_model._purpose: The 4 outcomes the report's stacked bars consume (green correct / orange wrong_kind / red omitted + insertion slice). Computed at MARK time from (target_form, chosen_form), not stored on the item — so one frame generates all outcomes.
outcome_model.omitted: chosen == target with ALL accents stripped. Covers meaning_pair picking the bare twin (è->e), tense_bearing picking the present twin (parlò->parlo), lexical_final citta, che_compound perche. This is why the accent-stripped twin is ALWAYS the primary distractor.
outcome_model.wrong_kind: chosen has an accent on the SAME vowel as target but the wrong MARK (grave<->acute): perché->perchè, and keyboard á/ó for à/ò (cittá). Carries BOTH chars per the Housing contract so the direction is recoverable.
outcome_model.inserted: chosen == target PLUS an accent the target lacks: qui->quì, sto->stò. In no_accent items this is the ONLY error direction; insertions attribute to the type TYPED (quì -> ì row).
outcome_model.EXCLUDED_not_an_accent_outcome: A chosen form that is a DIFFERENT valid word/form differing by more than an accent (parlerà for parlerò: different person, à vs ò) is a morphology choice, not an accent-mark error. See open_question Q1 — such person-pairs are held OUT of the clean frames.
outcome_model.false_alarm: RESOLVED 2026-07-21 (Smith): a learner who rejects a correct form simply couldn't confirm the spelling -> record as a MISS on that word/class (the same as failing to catch an error). No fifth bar, no capped generation, no overthinking. Judgement items generate freely.

credit_RULED_2026-07-21_Smith._scope: THE DRILL ONLY. Reverses never-dock FOR THIS DRILL (Smith, taxonomy thread v4). The live GRAMMAR-marker accent policy is unchanged: a grammar item still never docks for an accent slip (r29 banner). The dedicated drill docks because accents ARE what it tests.
credit_RULED_2026-07-21_Smith.surface_the_direction: Smith's instruction. Satisfied by the event contract: wrong_kind already carries BOTH chars (expected é / written è) so the acute<->grave direction shows in the verdict; omission/insertion show the missing/extra mark. The drill's feedback names it.

omitted_per_class_RULED._basis: ACCEPTED by Smith 2026-07-21. Smith left omission 'class-severity TBD'; this is exactly my §5 tiering (relayed by Architecture). Real-word-twin classes below baseline (omission changes the WORD/TENSE); misspelling classes at baseline (meaning recoverable). Average stays ~0.5, honouring his baseline.

frame_families.why_bare_works: In these classes the accent-stripped (or accent-added) twin is a NON-WORD / misspelling, so the correct spelling is decidable from the word alone. No carrier sentence needed — the pipeline permutes over the word axis for free. THIS is where the big volume comes from.

option_generation.lexical_final: target + stripped(target) [omitted] + wrongmark(target) where final vowel accentable both ways [wrong_kind]. e.g. città / citta / cittá.
option_generation.che_compound: target(acute) + gravemark(target) [wrong_kind, the hotspot] + stripped(target) [omitted]. e.g. perché / perchè / perche.
option_generation.no_accent: target(bare, CORRECT) + inserted(target) [inserted]. e.g. qui / quì. Correct answer is the BARE form; distractor is the temptation.
option_generation.apostrophe_not_accent: target(apostrophe) + accentform [inserted-as-accent] + bare. e.g. po' / pò / po.

frame_families.outcome_tagging: accent_type = the mark AT ISSUE (the vowel of the accent that is right, or for no_accent the vowel of the tempting accent). placement_class = the item's class. outcome computed per outcome_model.
frame_families.explanation_template: One line naming the fixed fact: e.g. "città always carries a grave accent on the final a (final-stressed noun); 'citta' and 'cittá' are both wrong." No rule-chip in the prompt (criterion 13 safe by construction — options are surface forms).

frame_families.shape: pick-the-form in a one-line carrier that FORCES one twin
frame_families.why_context_needed: Here the accent-stripped twin is ITSELF A REAL WORD (è/e, dà/da, parlò/parlo). Only the sentence disambiguates, so a carrier is mandatory. Carriers are hand-authored (below); the axis is (carrier x which-twin-is-keyed).
frame_families.outcome_tagging: target = the twin the carrier forces. Picking the other twin: if it is target-stripped -> omitted; if target is the bare word and they pick the accented -> inserted. accent_type = the accent on the accented member of the pair.
frame_families.explanation_template: Gloss the completed sentence in English (criterion 17) + one line on the twin: "'è' (with accent) is the verb 'is'; 'e' without the accent means 'and'."

frame_families.why: Speed. Design A2 names 'is this correct?' judgements explicitly. Highest items-per-minute.
frame_families.generation_constraint: Show EITHER the target (answer Yes; outcome=correct) OR a definite error-form (answer No; if learner wrongly says Yes, outcome = that error's type per outcome_model). Do NOT generate a 'reject the correct form' path beyond a capped rate — see open_question Q2.
frame_families.outcome_tagging: Shown error-form endorsed as correct -> that error's outcome. Error-form correctly rejected -> correct. Target endorsed -> correct.

meaning_pair.membership: CLOSED list (13). accent distinguishes two lexemes; twin is a real word.

carriers_force_accented.it: Ogni giorno lui mi ___ una mano.
carriers_force_accented.en: Every day he gives me a hand.
The book is there, on the table.
Wait for me there in front of the school.

carriers_force_bare.en: The tickets? I bought them yesterday.

carriers_force_accented.en: I want neither bread nor pasta.

carriers_force_bare.en: Cakes? I made two of them.
If it rains, I stay home.

carriers_force_accented.en: My dad works in a bank.

carriers_force_bare.en: The pope lives in the Vatican.

carriers_force_accented.en: Give me half of the cake.

carriers_force_accented.it: Hanno aperto un nuovo ___ a Venezia.
carriers_force_accented.en: They opened a new casino in Venice.

carriers_force_bare.it: In quella stanza c'era un gran ___.
carriers_force_bare.en: There was a real mess in that room.

pairs.note: RESOLVED 2026-07-21 (Smith): cross-tag, don't force. ciò has no atonic twin so it ALSO belongs to lexical_final (monosyllabic fixed accent); tag BOTH, flag as edge/weird. Two homes is Rev 27, not a problem.

RESOLVED 2026-07-21 (Smith): cross-tag + edge. dì(day) is literary AND the di'/dì/di TRIPLE spans apostrophe_not_accent; a triple item tags meaning_pair AND apostrophe_not_accent. Two homes, flagged weird.

carriers_force_accented.en: One distant day, I will return.

tense_bearing.membership: verb forms where the accent realises tense/person. Harvest from the verb_form item corpus + morphology. Twin is a real form.
tense_bearing.clean_frames_only: Options are {accented target, accent-stripped twin}. Picking the stripped twin = omitted. Pure PERSON-pairs (parlerò vs parlerà) are HELD OUT (open_question Q1).

carrier.it: L'anno scorso Dante le ___ a lungo.
carrier.en: Last year Dante spoke to her at length.

carrier.it: Quella sera Maria lo ___ senza parlare.
carrier.en: That evening Maria looked at him without speaking.
In 1945 my grandfather returned from the war.
Nobody believed her when she said it.
Tomorrow it will be a nice day.
Next year I will be thirty.
Who knows what the weather will be like tomorrow.

tense_bearing.note: futuro_accent_drop options are {sarà, sara}: sara is a NON-word (omitted). These are clean. It is only the {parlerò, parlerà} person-pair that is held out.

che_compound.membership: ends -ché (acute). Near-closed. Characteristic error is WRONG_KIND (perchè).

lexical_final.membership: final char in {à è é ì ò ù}, NOT meaning_pair / tense_bearing / che_compound. Productive (-ità) => harvest full list from vocab registry (survey found ~270 types).
lexical_final.primary_outcome: omitted (citta) then wrong_kind on the keyboard-á/ó cases (cittá).

no_accent.membership: CLOSED. Monosyllables/verb forms that must stay BARE; the error is INSERTED only.
no_accent.note: target is the BARE form (correct); distractor is the accented temptation (quì, stò, fù, sù...). Insertion attributes to the tempting vowel's type.

apostrophe_not_accent.membership: CLOSED. truncation apostrophe where an accent is tempting.

seed.gloss: give! (imperative) — the da'/dà/da TRIPLE is the richest site

declared_axes._note: What the pipeline permutes. Lighter than the pronoun aspettare battery — mostly a word axis + a fixed option-synthesis rule, not person/referent permutation.
acc_pick_bare: = no other axis; options synthesised by rule
acc_pick_context: (carrier x direction) pairs listed in class_seeds
later: more carriers per pair for volume

cross_credit_seams.stress: lexical_final words are pron_effect=stress_shift => shared with StressAuthor's final-accented class. Rev 27: an item where the learner both mis-accents AND the word's stress is the point can co-credit. Housing already asked to separate 'mis-stressed a shown word' from 'omitted accent hence mis-stressed' (their thread §2).
cross_credit_seams.vocab: lexical_final members (città, caffè...) are vocab lemmas. Getting the accented spelling right is 'knowing the word' — cross-credit to the vocabulary bucket per the pari-family precedent (DECISIONS 2026-07-20).

open_questions.q: tense_bearing PERSON-pairs (parlerò 1sg vs parlerà 3sg): both accented, differ by vowel+person. Not a clean accent-mark outcome. Proposal: HOLD OUT of the accent drill (they are future-formation discrimination — FutureFormationAuthor's leaf); keep only accent-DROP items (parlerò vs parlero) which are clean-omitted. Architecture to confirm the boundary.
open_questions.resolution: RESOLVED (Smith): parlerò/parlerà HELD OUT of the accent drill; keep only accent-drop items. Confirmed.

open_questions.q: acc_judge false-alarm: learner rejects a correct form. No home in the 4-outcome report. Proposal: record it as its own signal but do NOT surface in the stacked bars initially (cap generation of shown-correct judgement items low); revisit if Smith wants a false-alarm sliver. Housing/Architecture call.
open_questions.resolution: RESOLVED (Smith): false alarm = a miss ('can't spell it'). No new bar. See outcome_model.false_alarm.

open_questions.q: topic_short: tree root says 'accents', brief wants a short code. I used 'acc'. Confirm or normalise.

ciò and dì(day) placement: ciò has no atonic twin (behaves lexical_final); dì(day) is literary. Both currently parked in meaning_pair with notes. Move ciò to lexical_final? Keep dì low-priority? Author's lean: move ciò, keep dì flagged.
open_questions.resolution: RESOLVED (Smith): cross-tag ciò + dì (two homes, Rev 27) and flag edge/weird. Not forced into one class.

open_questions.q: Insertion credit value: Smith ruled wrong_kind 0.8 / omission 0.5 / correct 1.0 but not INSERTION. It is the only error for no_accent + apostrophe. Recommend 0.5 (definite error, baseline); alt 0.8 (mild wrong-instinct). Smith's call.
open_questions.resolution: ACCEPTED (Smith, 'the rest is fine'): insertion credit = 0.5.

open_questions.q: Per-class omission severity (Smith flagged 'class-severity TBD'). Proposed: meaning_pair + tense_bearing = 0.4 (omission changes the WORD/TENSE — my serious tier), che_compound + lexical_final = 0.5 (spelling slip, meaning recoverable). Average ~0.5 honours his baseline. Smith rules the gradient.
open_questions.resolution: ACCEPTED (Smith, 'the rest is fine'): per-class omission gradient meaning_pair/tense_bearing 0.4, che_compound/lexical_final 0.5.

edge_and_multihome_note: Smith 2026-07-21: placement classes are not mutually exclusive (Rev 27) — a word may tag two. The clean partition holds for the vast majority; the exceptions (ciò, dì, the da'/dà/da and di'/dì/di triples) are cross-tagged and flagged edge. If oddities accumulate, a small 'irregular/edge' grouping is Architecture's to mint (not minted here).
apostrophe_input_bug: Smith 2026-07-21: the apostrophe live-rewriter (housing/js/norm.js) auto-corrupts po' -> pO-grave (po' is NOT in the apocope allow-list va'/da'/fa'/sta'/di'); and the da'/di' reservation makes the commoner indicative dA-grave/dI-grave unreachable via the shortcut. Flagged to Housing with Smith's escalation fix (1 apostrophe=grave, 2=acute, 3=literal apostrophe) in inter_chat/AccentAuthor_Housing_apostrophe_input.md. Drill-adjacent (the drill is index-scored) but it would let the tool undermine the very lesson.
